etymology.description: 匕 (spoon) + 巛 (hair) + 囟 (fontanel) -> conception of brain elements -> 肉 (flesh) replaces 匕 for clarity -> 腦 (brain)
etymology.interpretation: The original character 匘 combined spoon-like 匕, hair-like 巛, and head-related 囟 to represent the brain's layered complexity. Later, 匕 was replaced with the more relevant 肉 (flesh), emphasizing the brain as a physical part of the body, leading to the modern character 腦 (nǎo, 'brain').
etymology.simplification: In simplified form, 脑, the complex components of 腦 were replaced with simpler forms to ease writing, retaining the essential semantic connection to flesh and head.

examples.chinese: 用脑好好思考(用腦好好思考)。
examples.english: Use your brain to think properly.

examples.chinese: 他有一个聪明的脑袋(他有一個聰明的腦袋)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǒu yīgè cōngmíng de nǎodai.

examples.chinese: 大脑是人体最重要的器官之一(大腦是人體最重要的器官之一)。
examples.pinyin: Dà nǎo shì réntǐ zuì zhòngyào de qìguān zhī yī.
examples.english: The brain is one of the most important organs in the human body.
